title: Bound States and Resonance Analysis of One-Dimensional Relativistic Parity-Symmetric Two Point Interactions
authors: Carlos A. Bonin, Manuel Gadella, José T. Lunardi, Luiz A. Manzoni
abstract: We consider the one-dimensional Dirac equation with the most general relativistic contact interaction supported on two points symmetrically located with respect to the origin. In order to determine the shape of the interaction, we use a distributional method, which in the present case is equivalent to the standard method of defining contact interactions by self-adjoint extensions of symmetric operators. The interaction on each of these two points depends on four parameters, each one having a clear physical meaning. We are interested in the scattering and confining properties of this model. We focus our attention on even or odd interactions under parity transformations and investigate the existence of critical and supercritical states, bound states, confinement and scattering resonances for some particular interactions of special interest.
summary: This paper provides a complete mathematical classification of the energy spectrum for a 1D Dirac particle interacting with two symmetric point-like barriers. It details how different types of relativistic interactions, such as scalar and electrostatic, lead to distinct phenomena like bound state absorption and the Klein effect.
main_problem: The study investigates the existence and properties of bound states, resonances, critical, and supercritical states in a 1D relativistic system with two symmetric point interactions.
main_result: The authors classify the energy spectrum for various parity-even and parity-odd arrangements, finding that scalar interactions admit bound states and resonances, while electrostatic interactions are non-confining and pseudoscalar interactions only admit resonances.
method: The paper uses a distributional approach and self-adjoint extensions to define contact interactions, employing transfer matrices and S-matrix pole analysis to determine the energy spectrum.
model_or_system: A one-dimensional Dirac equation for a particle of mass m, featuring two singular (contact) interactions located at x = +/- l/2, characterized by scalar, electrostatic, vector, and pseudoscalar parameters.
key_observables: Bound state energies, scattering resonances (S-matrix poles), transmission and reflection amplitudes, and critical/supercritical energy thresholds.
important_parameters: Interaction strengths (B, A0, A1, W), barrier separation (l), particle mass (m), and the parity (even/odd) of the arrangement.
main_assumptions: The system is restricted to one dimension and the interactions are assumed to be supported exactly at two symmetric points.
figures_summary: Plots showing the trajectory of resonances in the complex energy plane, the relationship between interaction strength and bound-state energies, and the movement of energies as parameters vary.
paper_structure: The paper establishes a mathematical framework for two-point relativistic interactions, analyzes parity-symmetric arrangements, investigates specific interaction types (scalar, electrostatic, etc.), and examines the limits of vanishing separation and non-relativistic behavior.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
